message:
fix(robot): deduplicate agents with case-insensitive name collisions

In multi-agent environments, the same logical agent can register
multiple rows when its name is spelled with different casing across
sessions (e.g., "RubyPrairie" vs "rubyprairie"). The build_agents()
query previously returned all rows, causing the TUI and robot views to
show duplicate entries for the same agent — one stale, one current.

The fix adds a NOT EXISTS anti-join that filters out older registrations
of the same agent name (using COLLATE NOCASE). Among duplicates, the row
with the highest last_active_ts wins; ties are broken by rowid. This
ensures the most recent registration is always the one displayed, while
older ghost rows are hidden.

The ORDER BY clause also gains a secondary sort on a.id DESC for
deterministic ordering when multiple agents share the same timestamp.

Includes a new unit test that creates two agents with the same name in
different casing, verifies that build_agents() collapses them to one
entry, and confirms the newer registration's program and message count
are used.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
